   119	        /// <summary>
   120	        /// Produce a Turnkey API stamp for <paramref name="payload"/>.
   121	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>ApiKeyStamper.stamp(payload)</c>.
   122	        /// </summary>
   123	        public StampResult Stamp(string payload)
   124	        {
   125	            if (payload == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(payload));
   126	
   127	            string signatureDerHex = SignWithApiKey(payload);
   128	
   129	            var stamp = new TurnkeyStamp
   130	            {
   131	                PublicKey = _apiPublicKey,
   132	                Scheme = SignatureScheme,
   133	                Signature = signatureDerHex,
   134	            };
   135	
   136	            string stampJson = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(
   137	                stamp, TurnkeyJsonContext.Default.TurnkeyStamp);
   138	
   139	            return new StampResult
   140	            {
   141	                StampHeaderName = StampHeaderName,
   142	                StampHeaderValue = Encoding.StringToBase64UrlString(stampJson),
   143	            };
   144	        }
   145	
    20	//
    21	// Wire bytes:
    22	//   - signature.toDERHex() output equals upstream's noble signature.toDERHex().
    23	//   - The stamp JSON shape is exactly the upstream's
    24	//     { publicKey, scheme, signature } in this order.
    25	//   - The X-Stamp header value is base64url(JSON), matching upstream's
    26	//     `stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp))`.
    27	//   - SHA-256(content) and deterministic ECDSA (RFC 6979) are used; low-S is
    28	//     enforced (noble defaults to lowS: true).
    29	
    30	using System;
    31	using System.IO;
    32	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
    33	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;
    34	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Signers;
    35	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
    36	using Org.BouncyCastle.Security;
    37	
    38	namespace Turnkey
    39	{
    40	    /// <summary>
    41	    /// Stamper for Turnkey API requests. 1:1 logical port of upstream
    42	    /// <c>@turnkey/api-key-stamper</c> at peak's pinned version 0.5.0.
    43	    /// </summary>
    44	    /// <remarks>
    45	    /// <para><b>Runtime parity target:</b> this port mirrors upstream's
    46	    /// <c>purejs</c> runtime (deterministic ECDSA via RFC 6979 + low-S).
    47	    /// Upstream's default <c>detectRuntime()</c> picks <c>"node"</c> in
    48	    /// Node which dispatches to <c>nodecrypto.ts</c> and produces
    49	    /// non-deterministic signatures (random k); behavior parity with
    50	    /// that runtime is intentionally NOT a goal — Turnkey's backend
    51	    /// accepts either, but the C# wire bytes will only match
    52	    /// deterministically against upstream <c>runtimeOverride: "purejs"</c>
    53	    /// callers. All three runtimes produce signatures that verify against
    54	    /// the same public key, so end-to-end Turnkey API calls work
    55	    /// regardless.</para>
    56	    /// <para><b>Validation timing:</b> the constructor parses but does NOT
    57	    /// validate the private key. Validation happens in
    58	    /// <see cref="SignWithApiKey(string)"/>, matching upstream which only
    59	    /// constructs noble's key object at sign time.</para>
    60	    /// </remarks>

    70	        }
    71	
    72	        /// <summary>
    73	        /// Create an <see cref="Http"/> client from an HPKE-encrypted Turnkey
    74	        /// credential bundle (the "legacy" flow used by export and bundle
    75	        /// auth recovery).
    76	        /// </summary>
    77	        /// <param name="encryptedCredentialBundle">
    78	        /// Base58Check-encoded HPKE bundle from Turnkey
    79	        /// (33-byte compressed sender key followed by the ciphertext).
    80	        /// </param>
    81	        /// <param name="targetPrivateKey">
    82	        /// Hex-encoded target P-256 private key (the receiver of the HPKE
    83	        /// envelope).
    84	        /// </param>
    85	        /// <param name="baseUrl">Optional Turnkey API base URL. Defaults to <see cref="DefaultBaseUrl"/>.</param>
    86	        public static Http GetHttpClient(
    87	            string encryptedCredentialBundle,
    88	            string targetPrivateKey,
    89	            string baseUrl = DefaultBaseUrl)
    90	        {
    91	            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(encryptedCredentialBundle))
    92	            {
    93	                throw new ArgumentException(
    94	                    "Encrypted credential bundle is required", nameof(encryptedCredentialBundle));
    95	            }
    96	            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(targetPrivateKey))
    97	            {
    98	                throw new ArgumentException(
    99	                    "Target private key is required", nameof(targetPrivateKey));
   100	            }
   101	
   102	            var apiPrivateKey = Crypto.DecryptCredentialBundle(encryptedCredentialBundle, targetPrivateKey);
   103	            var apiPrivateKeyBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(apiPrivateKey);
   104	            var apiPublicKeyBytes = Crypto.GetPublicKey(apiPrivateKeyBytes, isCompressed: true);
   105	
   106	            var normalizedPrivateKey = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(apiPrivateKeyBytes);
   107	            var apiPublicKey = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(apiPublicKeyBytes);
   108	
   109	            return new Http(new ApiKeyStamper(apiPublicKey, normalizedPrivateKey), baseUrl);
   110	        }
   111	
   112	        /// <summary>
   113	        /// Create an <see cref="Http"/> client directly from a target private
   114	        /// key (the OTP session flow where the session key is already known).
   115	        /// </summary>
   116	        /// <param name="targetPrivateKey">Hex-encoded target P-256 private key.</param>
   117	        /// <param name="baseUrl">Optional Turnkey API base URL. Defaults to <see cref="DefaultBaseUrl"/>.</param>
   118	        public static Http FromTargetPrivateKey(
   119	            string targetPrivateKey,
   120	            string baseUrl = DefaultBaseUrl)
   121	        {
   122	            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(targetPrivateKey))
   123	            {
   124	                throw new ArgumentException(
   125	                    "Target private key is required", nameof(targetPrivateKey));
   126	            }
   127	
   128	            var privateKeyBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(targetPrivateKey);
   129	            if (privateKeyBytes.Length == 0)
   130	            {
   131	                throw new ArgumentException(
   132	                    "Target private key was not valid hex", nameof(targetPrivateKey));
   133	            }
   134	
   135	            var normalizedPrivateKey = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(privateKeyBytes);
   136	            var publicKeyBytes = Crypto.GetPublicKey(privateKeyBytes, isCompressed: true);
   137	            var publicKeyHex = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKeyBytes);
   138	
   139	            return new Http(new ApiKeyStamper(publicKeyHex, normalizedPrivateKey), baseUrl);
   140	        }

   184	        public SignedRequest StampExportPrivateKey(ExportPrivateKeyRequestBody body)
   185	        {
   186	            if (body == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(body));
   187	            return CreateSignedRequest(
   188	                _baseUrl + "/public/v1/submit/export_private_key",
   189	                JsonSerializer.Serialize(body, TurnkeyJsonContext.Default.ExportPrivateKeyRequestBody));
   190	        }
   191	
   192	        /// <summary>
   193	        /// Build a signed <c>submit/export_wallet_account</c> request.
   194	        /// </summary>
   195	        public SignedRequest StampExportWalletAccount(ExportWalletAccountRequestBody body)
   196	        {
   197	            if (body == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(body));
   198	            return CreateSignedRequest(
   199	                _baseUrl + "/public/v1/submit/export_wallet_account",
   200	                JsonSerializer.Serialize(body, TurnkeyJsonContext.Default.ExportWalletAccountRequestBody));
   201	        }
   202	
   203	        private SignedRequest CreateSignedRequest(string url, string bodyJson)
   204	        {
   205	            var stampResult = _stamper.Stamp(bodyJson);
   206	            return new SignedRequest
   207	            {
   208	                Url = url,
   209	                Body = bodyJson,
   210	                Stamp = new Stamp

 succeeded in 0ms:
   410	            });

   413	
   414	        // ============================================================
   415	        // BuildAdditionalAssociatedData / FormatHpkeBuf
   416	        // ============================================================
   417	
   418	        [Fact]
   419	        public void BuildAdditionalAssociatedData_ConcatsArgs()
   420	        {
   421	            byte[] a = { 0x01, 0x02 };
   422	            byte[] b = { 0x03, 0x04, 0x05 };
   423	            byte[] aad = Crypto.BuildAdditionalAssociatedData(a, b);
   424	            aad.Should().Equal(new byte[] { 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 });
   425	        }
   426	
   427	        [Fact]
   428	        public void FormatHpkeBuf_ReturnsExpectedJson()
   429	        {
   430	            // Use a real HpkeEncrypt output so the bytes are valid.
   431	            var recv = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   432	            byte[] recvPubUncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(recv.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   433	
   434	            byte[] encrypted = Crypto.HpkeEncrypt(new Crypto.HpkeEncryptParams
   435	            {
   436	                PlainTextBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString("1234"),
   437	                TargetKeyBuf = recvPubUncompressed,
   438	            });
   439	
   440	            string json = Crypto.FormatHpkeBuf(encrypted);
   441	
   442	            // Shape contains exactly two keys in upstream order.
   443	            json.Should().StartWith("{\"encappedPublic\":\"04");
   444	            json.Should().Contain("\"ciphertext\":\"");
   445	            json.Should().EndWith("\"}");
   446	        }
   447	
   448	        [Fact]
   449	        public void FormatHpkeBuf_TooSmall_Throws()
   450	        {
   451	            // Upstream passes the slice to uncompressRawPublicKey which
   452	            // throws "failed to uncompress raw public key: invalid length"
   453	            // when the buffer is shorter than 33 bytes.
   454	            Action act = () => Crypto.FormatHpkeBuf(new byte[10]);
   455	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
   456	               .WithMessage("failed to uncompress raw public key: invalid length");
   457	        }
   458	
   459	        // ============================================================
   460	        // VerifySessionJwtSignature negative paths
   461	        // ============================================================
   462	
   463	        [Fact]
   464	        public void VerifySessionJwtSignature_Empty_Throws()
   465	        {
   466	            // Upstream: throws "invalid JWT: need 3 parts" when signature part is missing.
   467	            Action act = () => Crypto.VerifySessionJwtSignature(string.Empty);
   468	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   469	               .WithMessage("invalid JWT: need 3 parts");
   470	        }

src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

     1	# Turnkey upstream source pins
     2	
     3	The C# port targets the **peak-pinned** Turnkey npm versions, NOT the Unity
     4	port's newer versions. The npm tarball contents (extracted under
     5	[`upstream-snapshots/`](./upstream-snapshots/)) are the authoritative source.
     6	GitHub SHAs are secondary metadata.
     7	
     8	## Pin table
     9	
    10	| src C# file                | Upstream snapshot path                  | npm package                | Version | Tarball sha256 |
    11	|---|---|---|---|---|
    12	| src/Encoding.cs            | turnkey-encoding-0.6.0                  | @turnkey/encoding          | 0.6.0   | `2cf9e6ee1f47ac7e3cc3e644cdb0e3e112c906a6ea1af737777f4658b73fb7bc` |
    13	| src/CryptoConstants.cs     | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `75115706fccc29c664f9f918d9a0c2c1798eb03f261cb5b0c186c75663cf79d3` |
    14	| src/Crypto.cs              | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `75115706fccc29c664f9f918d9a0c2c1798eb03f261cb5b0c186c75663cf79d3` |
    15	| src/ApiKeyStamper.cs       | turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0           | @turnkey/api-key-stamper   | 0.5.0   | `962a2d22c7c40240f05be98533769b37ab7dad7dbb5abec762c41007233d02bd` |
    16	| src/Http.cs                | turnkey-http-3.16.0                     | @turnkey/http              | 3.16.0  | `d849f2156633f63062c52067785df1ce33eac0659044df932862c5d6de9dbdaf` |
    17	| src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs  | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | (no single upstream; covers DTOs from crypto + api-key-stamper + http) | n/a | n/a |
    18	
    19	Each upstream snapshot directory contains:
    20	
    21	- `package/` — verbatim npm tarball extract (the authoritative wire-format
    22	  source: `dist/*.js` + `dist/*.d.ts` + `package.json` + LICENSE / README).
    23	- `ts-source/` — the original TypeScript source from `github.com/tkhq/sdk` at
    24	  the matching git tag SHA (committed for human readability; the npm tarball
    25	  remains the wire-format source of truth if the two ever disagree).
    26	
    27	## Why these versions
    28	
    29	These are exactly what the peak monorepo pulls. See
    30	[`peak-lockfile-evidence.md`](./peak-lockfile-evidence.md) for the
    31	`pnpm-lock.yaml` lines.
    32	
    33	## GitHub commit SHAs (secondary)
    34	
    35	`gitHead` is **NOT present** in the published tarballs for these versions
    36	(`grep gitHead package/package.json` returns nothing for any of the four).
    37	The GitHub commit SHAs below are resolved from the git tag of the form
    38	`@turnkey/<pkg>@<version>` via `gh api /repos/tkhq/sdk/git/ref/tags/...`.
    39	These are recorded for cross-reference only. The npm tarball sha256 above
    40	is the controlling wire-format source.

**A. Source Pin**

- Upstream package: `@turnkey/crypto`
- Version: `2.8.8`
- Tarball sha256: `75115706fccc29c664f9f918d9a0c2c1798eb03f261cb5b0c186c75663cf79d3`
- C# file git SHA: `0f2029105aedbf353002ea2036538679fcfe3043`

**B. Method Coverage**

| C# method (file:line) | Upstream TS function (path:line) | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| N/A: no public/internal helper methods are declared in `src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs:55-66`; public member reviewed: `JsCompatibleEncoder` at `src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs:64-65` | N/A: C# source-generation infrastructure; relevant upstream JSON emitters are `crypto.ts:295`, `api-key-stamper/index.ts:102`, `http/base.ts:63` | REVIEWED | Generated context constructors/properties are compiler output, not helper methods declared in this file. |

**C. Intentional Adaptations**

- `JsonSerializerContext` source generation replaces JS runtime `JSON.stringify` calls for IL2CPP/AOT safety; structural when emitted bytes match.
- `[JsonSerializable]` registers every DTO serialized by owned call sites; structural metadata registration only.
- `[JsonPropertyName]` on DTOs in adjacent files preserves TS wire names despite C# PascalCase; no wire-name change.
- `WriteIndented = false` matches compact `JSON.stringify` output; no whitespace change.
- `DefaultIgnoreCondition = Never` plus per-property `WhenWritingNull` on optional `generateAppProofs` preserves required-field presence while omitting optional nulls.

**D. D17 Enforcement**

N/A (not Crypto.cs)

**E. Logic Divergence Findings**

| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs:38-41`, `src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs:64-65`; used by `ApiKeyStamper.cs:136-137`, `Crypto.cs:644`, `Http.cs:156/167/178/189/200` | `turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts:295-298`; `turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/index.ts:100-102`; `turnkey-http-3.16.0/ts-source/base.ts:63` | `JsCompatibleEncoder` is declared but not wired into `TurnkeyJsonContext.Default`. Current `System.Text.Json` default escaping can emit `\u003C`, `\u003E`, `\u0026`, and escaped non-ASCII where JS `JSON.stringify` emits literal `<`, `>`, `&`, and Unicode. ASCII-only hex/UUID fixtures are unaffected, but user-supplied strings such as names/IDs can produce different signed body bytes from Node. | Use an actual context/options instance with `JavaScriptEncoder.UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping` at all serializer call sites, or otherwise make the generated default context use that encoder. Add a Node byte fixture containing `<`, `>`, `&`, and non-ASCII strings. |

**F. Fixture Comparison Gate**

Not all fixture gates meet the requested “same bytes Node would produce” standard.

- `tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json` exercises `TurnkeyJsonContext` via `ApiKeyStamper.TurnkeyStamp`. It is pinned to upstream fixtures, and `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs:73-108` asserts decoded JSON key order and crypto-valid DER signature, but it intentionally does not assert exact Node header bytes because upstream Node signatures are nondeterministic.
- `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json` is pinned to `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8`, but the `TurnkeyJsonContext` path `FormatHpkeBuf_ReturnsExpectedJson` at `tests/CryptoTests.cs:428-446` is shape-only and generated locally, not a pinned Node byte fixture.
- No fixture currently covers the escaping edge case identified in E.

**G. Unresolved Assumptions**

- I did not run `dotnet test` or a Node fixture generator in this read-only sandbox.
- I could verify ASCII-shape parity statically, but exact Node byte parity for non-ASCII/HTML-sensitive JSON strings is not covered by the current fixtures.

Round 3 pass criterion is not met: B and D are satisfied, but E has one divergence and F is not “all fixtures match” under the requested byte-for-byte standard.

- Upstream package: `@turnkey/crypto`
- Version: `2.8.8`

**C. Intentional Adaptations**

- `JsonSerializerContext` source generation replaces JS runtime `JSON.stringify` calls for IL2CPP/AOT safety; structural when emitted bytes match.
- `[JsonSerializable]` registers every DTO serialized by owned call sites; structural metadata registration only.
- `[JsonPropertyName]` on DTOs in adjacent files preserves TS wire names despite C# PascalCase; no wire-name change.
- `WriteIndented = false` matches compact `JSON.stringify` output; no whitespace change.
- `DefaultIgnoreCondition = Never` plus per-property `WhenWritingNull` on optional `generateAppProofs` preserves required-field presence while omitting optional nulls.

N/A (not Crypto.cs)

**E. Logic Divergence Findings**

| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs:38-41`, `src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs:64-65`; used by `ApiKeyStamper.cs:136-137`, `Crypto.cs:644`, `Http.cs:156/167/178/189/200` | `turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts:295-298`; `turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/index.ts:100-102`; `turnkey-http-3.16.0/ts-source/base.ts:63` | `JsCompatibleEncoder` is declared but not wired into `TurnkeyJsonContext.Default`. Current `System.Text.Json` default escaping can emit `\u003C`, `\u003E`, `\u0026`, and escaped non-ASCII where JS `JSON.stringify` emits literal `<`, `>`, `&`, and Unicode. ASCII-only hex/UUID fixtures are unaffected, but user-supplied strings such as names/IDs can produce different signed body bytes from Node. | Use an actual context/options instance with `JavaScriptEncoder.UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping` at all serializer call sites, or otherwise make the generated default context use that encoder. Add a Node byte fixture containing `<`, `>`, `&`, and non-ASCII strings. |

**F. Fixture Comparison Gate**

Not all fixture gates meet the requested “same bytes Node would produce” standard.

- `tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json` exercises `TurnkeyJsonContext` via `ApiKeyStamper.TurnkeyStamp`. It is pinned to upstream fixtures, and `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs:73-108` asserts decoded JSON key order and crypto-valid DER signature, but it intentionally does not assert exact Node header bytes because upstream Node signatures are nondeterministic.
- `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json` is pinned to `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8`, but the `TurnkeyJsonContext` path `FormatHpkeBuf_ReturnsExpectedJson` at `tests/CryptoTests.cs:428-446` is shape-only and generated locally, not a pinned Node byte fixture.
- No fixture currently covers the escaping edge case identified in E.

**G. Unresolved Assumptions**

- I did not run `dotnet test` or a Node fixture generator in this read-only sandbox.
- I could verify ASCII-shape parity statically, but exact Node byte parity for non-ASCII/HTML-sensitive JSON strings is not covered by the current fixtures.

Round 3 pass criterion is not met: B and D are satisfied, but E has one divergence and F is not “all fixtures match” under the requested byte-for-byte standard.
